text.p: Class: M-VI
Color: Red (sub-dwarf)
Stellar Mass: 0.2
FTL Horizon: 0.18 AU
Habitable (zone 3): Dwarf Planetoid, 0.16 AU
Class: M-II
Color: Red (bright giant)
Stellar Mass: 0.3 x 25.3
Near (zone 1): Empty Zone
Inner (zone 2): Empty Zone
Habitable (zone 3): Empty Zone
Outer (zone 4): Empty Zone
Outer (zone 5): Empty Zone
Outer (zone 6): Empty Zone
Far (zone 7): Empty Zone
Far (zone 8): Jovian: Ice (If zone 1, change to Jovian: Gas)
Far (zone 9): Jovian: Ice (If zone 1, change to Jovian: Gas)
Far (zone 10): Jovian: Gas
Zone: Habitable
Orbit: 0.16 AU
Gravity: 0.87 g
Diameter: 0.11 (1,403 km)
4406 km circumference
Mass: 0.022
# Moons: 1
Rings: None
Axial Tilt: 2 degrees
Length of Day: Typical length of day 18 hours
Length of Year: Typical (345 days)
% Water: 17
Toxicity: Trace Hazard
Pressure: Normal, no hazard
Toxicity: Severe Hazard
Radiation: Trace Hazard
Temperature: Normal, no hazard
Dominant Terrain: Jungle, Marsh
Dominant Lifeform: Animal
Swathes of lush rain-forest dominate the equatorial regions, which transitions to marsh and broken plains near the poles.
Gravity: 0.87 g
Diameter: 0.16
# Moons: Dwarf Planetoid Moons
Rings: None
Class: M-VI
Color: Red (sub-dwarf)
Stellar Mass: 0.2
FTL Horizon: 0.18 AU
Habitable (zone 3): Dwarf Planetoid, 0.16 AU

tr.td: Swathes of lush rain-forest dominate the equatorial regions, which transitions to marsh and broken plains near the poles.

gmnotes.p: This was one of the planetary definse sites that was destroyed by a targeted kinetic meteor that destroyed the facility.

text.p: Situated in a large meadow near the foothills of a regional mountain chain. This location was selected as a dig site as orbital scans indicated an unnatural crater centered at this location, with large concentrations of rare isotopes, that was later filled by erosion.

Institute structures Includes a large covered dome (not fully enclosed) where excavation occurs in the summers. Several outbuildings including a garage and two living quarters. Research indicates it was an agricultural trade center. Explanation for the isotopes is inconclusive.
Currently “closed” up for winter, no sign of recent activities Explorer and Bots are in long term storage and can be re-activated.

gmnotes.p: Summer site and where Team Alpha is expected to be. Shows signs of aerial assault. Explorer missing.

text.p: This site sits on a hill above a river. It includes underground structures that may have been either hydro-power generation or a sub-surface water supply network.
Institute structures include a connected set of small residences near the hill top, a protective building and laboratory over the sub-structure entrance, and a vehicle garage at the hill base.

gmnotes.p: Currently just emerging from winter season and has not yet been activated by the Institute’s team.

text.p: Named for an exploration team member who died. This site sits in the marshes near the coast and consists of numerous ruined structures including several large hardened pads and what may have been landing strips. This was the first site identified and is believed to have been a spaceport.
Institute structures include a VTOL landing facility, residences, and both land and water vehicle storage.

gmnotes.p: This was one of the planetary definse sites that was destroyed by a inter-planetary war.
An emitter and control center for a planetary defense shield that provied a global Guass screen for the planet. It now powers the massive ElectroMagnetic storm

text.p: An unsual domes structure that is made out of poly compounds. The structure has no metals in it's construction and appears to have no means of entry.

p.i: “Looks like a cargo hauler mated with a war beast. Poor eyesight, sure—but once it locks on, you’re either airborne or underground.” — Captain Vorn

notes.p: The Triofant is a massive quadrupedal creature, standing nearly 3 meters tall at the shoulder and weighing upwards of 6 metric tons. Its massive body is covered in thick, leathery, and mottled in shades of slate gray and moss green skin, often caked with mud or foliage from its territory. Its head is protected by a wide, bony frill reinforced with overlapping chitinous plates, and three forward-facing horns—two above the eyes and one central—used for ramming and flipping threats.
Despite its poor eyesight, the Triofant possesses acute olfactory and auditory senses, allowing it to track prey or intruders with unnerving precision.

list.li: Territorial Guardian: Generally passive unless its domain is breached or its young are threatened.
Cunning Predator (When Provoked): Will stalk and ambush perceived threats using terrain and cover.
Memory-Based Aggression: Remembers scents and sounds associated with past conflicts—may attack specific vehicles or color schemes.
Group Dynamics: Occasionally travels in small herds; lone bulls are more aggressive.

notes.p: More about static defense of a location rather than active patrolling or assault. Their threat level comes from their presence and numbers.
A citizen-soldier, often with basic training but strong motivation (e.g., defending their home). Their capability can vary widely.
A citizen-soldier, often with basic training but strong motivation (e.g., defending their home). Their capability can vary widely.
A citizen-soldier, often with basic training but strong motivation (e.g., defending their home). Their capability can vary widely.
Seeking, scanning, finding
Standard
Travel: 800kph
When players are searching for someone they cannot find, they turn to bounty hunters. When bounty hunters are searching for someone they cannot find, they turn to seeker bots.

These amazing robots are tiny and capable. They can be mistaken for a large hunting bird and move about by repulsor jet technology, able to fly at impressive speeds. They have such an array of scanners available that they can find almost anyone who is able to be found.

Seeker bots have an integrated laser, but due to their violence interlocks they will only use it as a tool or against robots or structures impeding their investigations (though they have no Marksman training to fire it well!). Seeker bots are often owned by banks, law enforcement, bounty hunters, espionage agencies, and governments.
Survival, reconnaissance, navigation
Removed
Travel: 10kph

Not all missions a player is sent on take place in the urban sprawls of the galaxy. Some take place on the vast wildernesses of remote worlds or unexplored fertile moons. Scout bots know how to keep people alive in the wilds. Characters who intend to spend a lot of time in the wild could do worse than buy a scout bot.

Scout bots have modified violence interlocks and won’t harm a sapient being, though they will kill non-sapient biological animals in order to feed those in their charge. They have advanced detection algorithms to determine if an animal they’ve just encountered for the first time is sapient or if it’s potential food for his owner and team.
Scout bots make excellent protectors and guides.
Someone quick and agile, good at hit-and-run tactics, but potentially vulnerable in sustained engagements.
A soldier skilled in stealth and observation over direct confrontation, focused on gathering information.

text.p: This site sits on a hill above a river. It includes underground structures that may have been either hydro-power generation or a sub-surface water supply network.
Institute structures include a connected set of small residences near the hill top, a protective building and laboratory over the sub-structure entrance, and a vehicle garage at the hill base.

Situated in a large meadow near the foothills of a regional mountain chain. This location was selected as a dig site as orbital scans indicated an unnatural crater centered at this location, with large concentrations of rare isotopes, that was later filled by erosion.

Institute structures Includes a large covered dome (not fully enclosed) where excavation occurs in the summers. Several outbuildings including a garage and two living quarters. Research indicates it was an agricultural trade center. Explanation for the isotopes is inconclusive.
Currently “closed” up for winter, no sign of recent activities Explorer and Bots are in long term storage and can be re-activated.

Named for an exploration team member who died. This site sits in the marshes near the coast and consists of numerous ruined structures including several large hardened pads and what may have been landing strips. This was the first site identified and is believed to have been a spaceport.
Institute structures include a VTOL landing facility, residences, and both land and water vehicle storage.
Read, share or paraphrase the following for the party at this time;
text.frame: After arriving at the Nexus Institute’s off-campus facility and passing through noticeable security, the party is shown to a conference room. Inside is a late middle aged human male who turns to greet the party. Standing at the back of the room watching closely are two figures dressed in grey suits and wearing sunglasses, one a human female, the other a male yar. The friendly man introduces himself as Dr. Vayden and with a uncomfortable smile introduces the two figures in grey suits as Ms. Johnson & Mr. Johnson.

"Ancient ruins on the planet being investigated by what is referred to as the Alpha Team. While returning from a regular resupply run, the Archaeon Scholar, the Alpha Team’s ship, detected a ship arrival in system from further in the nebula. The ship was of alien design. The Scholar attempted contact and approached. The alien ship fired upon Scholar and then may have had difficulties as it’s quantum coil detonated. The design language of this ship matches records of an alien ship that was encountered near the Gravity Nexus in 99 GFT, that ship also exploded when approached.

text.p: All of the stories (or the report images if preferred) in the sections "Nagol Briefing Packet" and "Julianos Breifing Packet" should be shared with the players at this time. Dr. Vayden will give the group some time to review the materials and answer any questions they may have.
When questions have been answered, proceed to The Second Briefing .
Refer to the section Frequently Asked Questions as needed.

linklist.link: Story: Frequently Asked Questions and Answers

text.frame: “This event is what caused the recent rumors. Given the hostile response from the alien ship, the Institute has brought in the GTF. Who has, wisely, placed security around the program until the seriousness of the event can be determined.”

"The Nexus Institute has sent a second ship, the Pathfinder, and team, Beta, to backtrack the path of the alien ship. Beta team has been given recognition codes that we will also give to you and you can expect to render aid to one another as needed. Given the nature of the nebula, no communications have been possible with Beta. But, a few days prior to the Institute contacting the players, the Omega Beacon from the Alpha team in the Nagol system was activated. Other than a ship board explosion and then the detonation of Scholar’s Nova Coil, no other activity was logged."
“Please take some time to review your packets, then I will answer any questions you may have.”

Once more with a strained smile Dr. Vayden continues. "Discovered by my team almost 2 years ago, we found a star system in the Nagol Dust Cloud. A system with a red dwarf, including a habitable planet. We named the system Nagol, to confuse any references to it. And the planet Julianos. Please see the data packets on the secure datapads."

text.p: Omega Beacons are often mounted to the exterior of a ship, or in this case dropped in space to monitor, and when a ship’s Nova coil explodes, they return with ~7 days of sensor data to a pre-programmed location. Omega Beacons are standard practice for all Institute ships on explorations in unsettled systems. A beacon will be installed upon the party’s ship.
Alpha team is 15 people. 10 scientists, 1 pilot, 1 technician and 3 wilderness guides.
Dr. Aris Thorne : Lead Xenobotanist, obsessed with alien flora's potential for medicine.
Dr. Lena Petrova: Astrogeologist, specialized in exotic planetary formations and subsurface scanning.
Dr. Kenji Tanaka: Chief Archaeolinguist, dedicated to deciphering ancient alien scripts and languages.
Dr. Anya Sharma: Exoplanetary Biologist, focused on extremophile life and adaptation in harsh environments.
Dr. Marcus "Mac" O'Connell: Cultural Anthropologist, known for his ability to synthesize disparate data into coherent narratives.
Dr. Chloe Dupont: Quantum Physicist, responsible for analyzing energy signatures and exotic matter.
Dr. Javier "Javi" Ramos: Astrominerologist, an expert in identifying valuable and unusual alien mineral deposits.
Dr. Elise Vandenberg: Paleo-Ethnobotanist, researching the interaction between ancient civilizations and their plant-based resources.
Dr. Omar Hassan: Computational Archaeologist, using advanced algorithms to reconstruct ancient structures from fragmented data.
Dr. Li Mei: Atmospheric Chemist, studying alien atmospheric compositions for habitability and unique properties.
Captain Jax "Ghost" Ryder: The Archaeon Scholar's pilot, renowned for his stealth navigation and calm under pressure.
Tech Specialist Kaelen Vance: Lead Field Technician, capable of repairing virtually any equipment under extreme conditions.
Sergeant Varrus "Tracker" Stone: Head Wilderness Guide, an ex-military survival expert with unparalleled tracking skills.
Lyra "Shade" Kael: Silent and observant, specializing in low-impact reconnaissance and environmental hazard assessment.
Rix "Pathfinder" Borin: A seasoned explorer with an intuitive sense for navigating complex and dangerous alien terrains.

The Scholar is a small class exploration starship similar to a typical scout ship and with atmospheric capabilities. Armed with a single laser battery and civilian screens. It is equipped with transit type quarters for up to twenty, science labs and a full medical bay, and has a moderate sized cargo bay
Beta Team of 12 people; 2 pilots, 4 gunners, 2 technicians, 2 medics, 2 scientists
Captain Eva Rostova: The Pathfinder's lead pilot, known for her aggressive yet precise flight maneuvers in hazardous conditions.
Lieutenant Kaito "Drifter" Nakamura: A secondary pilot with an uncanny knack for navigating dense nebulae and unpredictable gravitational anomalies.
Sergeant "Blaze" Morales: A crack shot with heavy weapon systems, specializing in target acquisition in chaotic environments.
Corporal Lyra "Sharp" Chen: Agile and highly observant, she excels at defensive fire and pinpointing weak spots on enemy craft.
Specialist Rex "Ironhide" Bronson: A stoic and dependable gunner, responsible for the vehicle's integrity and sustained suppressive fire.
Private Zara "Quickdraw" Khan: Young but exceptionally reactive, she handles rapid-fire armaments and evasive countermeasures.
Lead Tech Jax "Spark" Sterling: Brilliant and resourceful, he keeps the Pathfinder's intricate systems running, no matter the damage.
Field Engineer Lena "Wrench" Volkov: Expert in structural repairs and jury-rigging solutions in the field, often under duress.
Chief Medic Dr. Aris Thorne: Calm under fire, he's a veteran combat medic capable of advanced trauma care in any situation.
Paramedic Finn O'Malley: Quick-thinking and empathetic, he specializes in rapid triage and stabilization on the front lines.
Dr. Evelyn Reed: Astrogator and Data Analyst, tasked with deciphering the alien ship's trajectory and any lingering data trails.
Dr. Rohan Patel: Xenobio-Forensicist, focused on identifying any biological residue or environmental impact left by the alien vessel.

The Pathfinder is more akin to a corvette and is a small paramilitary class ship with atmospheric capabilities. Something the Institute uses in high-risk endeavors. Armed with an array of 4 weapon systems and military grade screens. It has quarters for 12 and fuel capacity for 40 light years of Nova travel.

The planet has a solid bio spectrum of life. No unusual pathogens or toxins have yet been detected, but a full biological survey has not been conducted. Only limited surveys at the archaeological sites

The two most dangerous creatures that have been encountered are the Triofant and Velvrax identified in the briefing materials. . The triofants are a large herding animal that have been observed on the plains in large numbers. They have not show aggressive behavior at a distance. Signs of the velvrax have been observed multiple times, but only one image was captured via a remote sensor. The creatures are assumed to be cunning predators and caution should be taken if they are encountered.

The team’s three wilderness guides are all exceptionally capable and experienced. The senior scientists have all been on planetary expeditions before. While all team members have received exo-wilderness training. The team should be able to survive on planet for months if not years.

Once you are deep in the Dust Cloud, you will be out of communications. Currently the agents have no authorization to establish another mission team if the party is overdue or the omega beacon is activated and returns to Daegon. But, whatever data it returns would be evaluated at that time and a decision made.

The Beta team is expected to pass through the Nagol system when it’s mission is completed. Both the party and the Beta team have been given recognition codes and are expected to assist one another within mission parameters.

text.h: What's the Federation's Theory on the Aliens
What is Known About any Collaboration with the Aliens

text.p: When the party is finished asking questions about Dr. Vayden's briefing, Mr. Johnson asks the Doctor to leave, stating that they will visit him in his lab before they leave.
After Dr. Vayden leaves and the door closes, Ms. Johnson starts speaking;
text.frame: "Alright, listen up. The GTF has some additional information to share with you. All of this is highly restricted, but I've managed to convince my superior that a high-level summary might be necessary for your success.
"Over the past 18 months, the GTF has received reports of five other incidents involving unknown ships that share a similar design language.

"For instance, we have sensor data of a matching alien ship jumping out of the GII-8 system, over by the Queen’s Lights. There's also a report of another matching alien ship refueling in the MV-1A system, in the Void Nexus. And here's where it gets interesting: a GTF patrol ship actually encountered a matching alien ship refueling at the gas giant in the Firestar system. That ship, just like the one in the Nagol system, experienced a quantum coil failure.

"We also have partial data from a Frontier ship that was illegally exploring the FV-9 system near Ken’Rhael. They transmitted a subspace distress call, and in that, they included a description of an alien ship. And most concerning, an alien ship was seen rendezvousing with an unmarked Frontier ship in the MV-5J system, near Neriph, less than two months ago.

"Now, the first three ships we encountered, they were all relatively small. If we're comparing them to humanoid technology, we estimate crews of four to eight, and they appeared to be lightly armed, if at all. We suspect they might be long-range scout craft. The FV-9 ship was apparently more well-armed and may have been larger; unfortunately, we only have a brief description that doesn't include specific size or tactical information. The ship in MV-5J, however, was certainly larger. It appeared capable of carrying a sizeable amount of cargo, or perhaps even smaller starships like those seen in the other incidents.

"In addition to your mission for the Nexus Institute, we need you to collect any data on these aliens you can. We're talking about ship capabilities—weapons and defenses, FTL technology—as well as their biology, motivations, and the cause for this apparent suicide behavior with their quantum coils. They pose a significant risk to the entire Frontier, and their outright refusal to communicate with the Institute’s team only heightens our level of concern."

Pausing for emphasis and to make sure she has everyone's full attention. “So, let me be clear, collecting intelligence on these aliens, if you encounter them, and returning it to us is your first priority. Rescue the scientists if you can, but these aliens pose a risk to millions, we have to know more.”
“Now's your chance to ask questions, if I can answer them”, she finishes.
text.p: There is not much more she can say than what is in the previous questions and Answers section, except to help clarify locations of the encounters on a star map. Other than the first event being "about 18 months ago" and the last "less than two months" she actually knowns very little detail.

Team of 7 (was 8): 1 commander (H), 1 medic (H), 2 scouts (Y), 1 technician(H), 2 gunners (H,N). Raided & mostly destroyed Aerum. 2 scout bots w/ assault rifles (no violence interlocks), 1 seeker bot (no violence interlock),
2 Hover Cars
The Void Enclave group has detected the party via survaillence by their seeker bot and has aranged an ambush at a river crossing where a herd of triofants are watering.

Will use a grenade launcher to try to agitate the herd and drive a few of the bulls to attack the party. This has a 20% chance of driving the bulls toward the party, 20% towards the ambushers, 20% to drive them away and 40% chance of simple confusing them Each subsequent turn the chance of them moving away increases by 20%.
Party starts on the east. Seeker bot does not engage and will try to return to the crash site where the rest of the Enclave team is located.

First turn storms gathers and lighting crackles near the ship. Second turn lightning strikes begin, Pilot-AGL or lightning strike. Each turn after gets harder to avoid, -5 penalty each turn. After -60 penalty halves but now two check. Rpeats to four at -60, etc.

When entering second hex of travel into it, the storm starts to react. Visible roiling clouds and internal lightning seems to be coelescing over the ship. Science-INT to determine storm is reacting to ship and will soon target the ship.
Third-fourth hex lighting strikes, Piot-AGL to avoid
Fifth plus hex lightning strikes, as the storm Pilot-AGL to avoid. Storm gets worse -5 to check every 2 hexes utnil _20. Continues until leaves the storm region.
4D BP, Any 1's or 2's rolled on damage causes Specific Damage
Will try to wait until the team exits their vehicle and gets to the crashed ship.
2 people and 4 security robots in ship. Others in surrounding hills.

The Nexus Institute is an academic organization of higher learning and research in the Galactic Federation located on Daegon Prime . The Institute is renowned for its work in the fields of archeology, pre-collaboration Frontier history, and planetary sciences. They and their staff participate in numerous explorations throughout the Frontier and are well known for their contributions to projects in the Stygia and Neriph systems. More recently there has been some public reports of an exploration team led by Dr. Elias Vayden being active in the Nagol Dust Clouds .

These explorations by Dr. Vayden have been ongoing for over ten years as they have worked to map starship navigable paths through the Dust Clouds with the hope of finding commercial paths through the nebula or identifying the location of the wormhole the first yar settlers used.

The adventure begins when the player characters are hired by the prestigious Nexus Institute for what appears to be a high-stakes search-and-rescue mission. They are tasked with traveling to the newly discovered planet Julianos , hidden deep within the treacherous Nagol Dust Cloud, to find the Institute’s missing "Alpha Team," a 15-person scientific crew led by the experienced Dr. Aris Thorne .

The briefing reveals a complex situation: Alpha Team's ship, the Archaeon Scholar, reported a hostile encounter with an unknown alien vessel before it mysteriously self-destructing. Shortly after, the Scholar itself was destroyed, triggering an emergency beacon—the party's only lead.

The mission takes a conspiratorial turn when the party is pulled into a secondary, confidential briefing with Ms. Johnson of the Galactic Task Force (GTF). She reveals that the "unknown alien" encounter is part of a disturbing pattern of similar ships appearing across the Frontier. The GTF believes these aliens, who seem to prefer suicide to capture, pose a significant threat. The party's true objective is now twofold: rescue any survivors of Alpha Team, but more importantly, gather any and all intelligence on this new alien threat.

Upon arriving at Julianos, the party discovers a dangerous world wracked by massive, semi-permanent electromagnetic storms that render flight and communications nearly impossible. They find Alpha Team’s main research site, Aevum , brutally attacked and destroyed, not by an accident or a strange alien, but by a professional, well-equipped para-military pirates.

The mission shifts from rescue to a tense investigation and survival scenario. By following clues—such scientific records, vehicles tracks, and weather anomalies—the players may learn some of the secrets the planet Julianos holds, rescue the surviving scientists, and learn clues that may lead to a more sinister truth hiding in the Nagol Dust Cloud.
The players ultimate goal is to survive the hostile planet and the surviving pirates, rescue the scientists, and escape with the clues to a more dangerous discovery.
If you are not jumping right in to this adventure with your campaign, you can plant the seeds of the upcoming adventure with the following rumors or news stories.

Reports of celebrations and academic accolades have suddenly sprung up from a research team from the Nexus Institute on Daegon Prime. Though unconfirmed, it is being said that the team recently returned from a deep exploration into the Nagol Dust clouds and have returned with astonishing findings. Though the institute will confirm none of the rumors, they include the discovery of an artifact of the original Yar colonization fleet, speculation on evidence of a fifth Frontier species, and of course, the possibility of a stable wormhole.

Dr. Elias Vayden, a senior researcher at the Institute has publicly stated, “The Nexus Institute appreciates the public interest in our work. What we do is important not only to science but to all the being of the Frontier. Though we are happy and excited for the return of our long-term research team, as we always are when any of our colleagues undertake such a risk filled journey into the Dust and return safely, we have nothing else to report at this time. It is always critical that our research go through full scientific investigation and review before publishing any potential discoveries for public consideration and wider scientific review. “

“Our teams always bring back data that is exciting and interesting to those of us who study the minutia of the Dust Clouds and the Nexi which is essential to our growing understanding, but rarely are such things of interest to those outside our fields of study. Our research is critical to the Frontier, but it takes place in tiny increments, building upon decades of research and study. There is no eureka moment or discovery. Once again, the Institute thanks everyone for their interest.”

text.h: Fed Activities at the Nexus Institute
text.p: Rumors continue to emerge from the Nexus Institute including speculation of the arrival of a Galactic Federation science team. No further official statements have been made by either the Institute or the Feds, but unusual laboratory lock downs, new security processes, and a seeming turn-over in staffing has fueled continued speculation.

text.h: Pale Eidolon Returns? Pirate Legend Spotted in Three Systems—Or Three Timelines?
text.p: The ghost ship Pale Eidolon—bane of trade routes and darling of conspiracy feeds—has allegedly resurfaced in Danasia, Krighton, and Stygia systems within a 72-hour window. Sensor logs are tantalizing but inconclusive, and some theorists suggest the sightings may not be of the same vessel… or even the same era. Tied to the recent gravitonic rupture near Vlasa-Kai, speculation runs wild: has the Eidolon slipped through a time rift, or are we witnessing the birth of a multiversal fleet? Whatever the truth, spacers are advised to steer clear—and keep their quantum anchors calibrated.
There are many ways for the player characters to be engaged with this adventure.

list.li: Due to the party’s reputation or a referral from a former employer, a discreet liaison from the Nexus Institute approaches the party. The offer is simple and lucrative: a high-risk, high-reward contract.

One of the player characters has a personal connection to a member of the missing team. Perhaps they served in the military with Sergeant Varrus "Tracker" Stone, learned to pilot from Captain Jax "Ghost" Ryder, or grew up with the resourceful technician Kaelen Vance. A frantic family member or mutual friend contacts the party, explaining that their friend is missing and the Nexus Institute is being evasive. They pool their life savings to hire the party to simply find out what really happened. When the party starts asking questions, they draw the attention of the GTF, who decides to recruit these motivated individuals for the official mission. The party owes a high ranking officer of the Galatic Task Force, and they are calling in that favor now.

The party owes a significant debt to a high-ranking official within the GTF—perhaps Ms. Johnson herself or her superior. This official made a past crime, a legal entanglement, or a disastrous mistake disappear for them. Now, it's time to pay up. The official contacts them with an offer they can't refuse: "volunteer" for a critical intelligence-gathering mission under the guise of a rescue operation. Refusal means the evidence of their past transgressions will suddenly resurface.

While conducting their own business (be it smuggling, salvage, or exploration) near the MV-5J system, the party's ship detects a strange energy signature. They witness the secret rendezvous mentioned by the GTF: a bizarre, unknown alien ship meeting with an unmarked Frontier vessel. Before they can process what they've seen, they are spotted and have to flee. Now, they are a loose end. They possess the only direct evidence of the conspiracy and are likely being hunted by the Void Enclave. Their only hope for survival is to take their data to the GTF, who will immediately recognize its significance and recruit them for the mission to Nagol—the one place they might get answers.

text.p: Note, if the players do not have their own starship, the Nagol Institute will provide a standard Scout starship and pilot/navigator (if needed).
text

id-00002.keywords: base garage includes vehicle hydro-power entrance sub-structure near water laboratory supply building structures hill either aevum include institute network set underground top river residences sits site small generation connected may sub-surface protective

id-00001.name: The Grey Agents' Briefing

exceptionally archaeologist uses data scholar frequently ex-military structures equipped tanaka nebulae rapid-fire quarters unpredictable adaptation pre-programmed alpha narratives flora's archaeolinguist dedicated pilots ancient o'connell calm pathfinder's numbers codes team case anomalies scout vehicle's members solid authorization reconnaissance theory explorations received training id-00004.name: The Hook(s)

id-00001.name: Hover Car

notes.p: Hover cars are quite common in the more settled regions of the frontier. On the heavily citied worlds of the central frontier, they are the preferred modes of transport. Hover cars appear like normal ground cars but lack wheels. Instead, they have a padded hover skirt under which rests either large air fans (which blow air downward with sufficient power to generate lift) or repulsor lifts. Other than their lower maximum speeds and poorer maneuverability, a hover car is very much like a hover cycle. The advantage of a hover car, however, is the canopied crew compartment, which provides protection to those within.

id-00001.primaryfunction: A hovering vehicle common in civilized centers of the frontier. They hover with large hoverfans or by repulsor technology and can reach impressive speeds.
